key_findings: Rules outperform ML on 84-case synthetic set (91.7% vs 84.5%) because synthetic set is optimised for rule structure
V4 catches 0/16 small_value_error ambiguous cases — signal space cannot distinguish grounded-correct from grounded-3%-off
max_relative_error has near-zero coefficient (0.133) — not discriminative in current signal schema
pnl_identity_fail_count is strongest discriminator for REPAIR class (coefficient 2.228)
scale_mismatch_count is strongest discriminator for FLAG class (coefficient 1.908)
5 zero-variance features identified — dropped in V4b with no accuracy impact
Near-tolerance signal gap identified — future work
architecture_conclusion: The hybrid rule+ML design is validated. Rules handle clear cases with 91.7% accuracy and zero false flags. ML adds interpretable feature importance showing domain constraints (identity_fail, scale_mismatch) are more discriminative than general grounding (coverage_ratio) for classification. The near-tolerance ambiguous zone requires a dedicated signal in V5.

future_work.near_tolerance_signal: Add near_tolerance_flag that fires when max_relative_error is between 0.5x and 2x tolerance — this would give the model information to classify financially-material but numerically-close errors
future_work.larger_ambiguous_tier: Expand ambiguous tier to 200+ cases with second annotator for inter-annotator agreement measurement
future_work.real_llm_errors: Collect actual LLM-generated wrong answers from GPT-4/Claude on real P&L questions for authentic error distribution
